title: mentha longifolia subsp. syriaca
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. syriaca, also known as Syrian mint or long-leaved mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce pain. The health benefits of Syrian mint include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, improve respiratory health, and even exhibit potential anti-cancer properties. Syrian mint can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, oil, and tincture, which can be used to support overall well-being and provide relief from various health issues.
